purpose: Fidelity evaluator: can a judge recover each schema dimension from the activity bundle alone? Mirror dimensions 1-32 with one question each (some richer dimensions get 2-3).

scoring_rules.exact_match: Normalized case-insensitive string match → 1.0 if equal else 0.0
scoring_rules.numeric_tolerance: 1.0 if |answer - truth| ≤ tolerance; linear falloff to 0 at 2× tolerance
scoring_rules.set_f1: F1 = 2·P·R/(P+R) over predicted vs ground-truth set (string-normalized)
scoring_rules.ordinal_bin: 1.0 if same bin; 0.5 if adjacent bin; 0.0 if 2+ bins away
scoring_rules.rubric_3band: 0 = wrong category; 0.5 = related/adjacent; 1.0 = exact category match

hardness_scale.2: Recoverable from a few activities (age, work schedule)
hardness_scale.3: Multi-event correlation needed (OCEAN moderate, secondary MICE+V)

aggregate.per_dimension: mean across the 1-3 questions tied to each dimension

questions.question: What is the subject's age in years as of the activity period? Provide an integer.
questions.notes: Recoverable from generational markers, life events, references to school years.

questions.question: What is the subject's gender? One of: M, F, NB, other.
questions.notes: Often visible from pronouns, names, gendered events.

questions.question: In which city does the subject live? Provide the city name only.
questions.notes: Direct from location events. Normalize case + spelling.

questions.question: In which country does the subject live? Provide ISO-style country name.
questions.notes: Country-alias normalisation (USA = United States = US).

questions.question: What is the subject's socioeconomic tier? One of: low, lower-mid, mid, upper-mid, high.
questions.notes: Inferable from spending patterns, neighborhood, vocation.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary vocation category? One of: tech, healthcare, finance, legal, education, military, government, retail, creative, manual, student, retired, other.
questions.notes: Direct from work-related events, calendar, messages.

questions.question: What is the subject's political orientation? One of: far-left, left, center, right, far-right, none-apparent.
questions.notes: From media consumption, donations, watched videos.

questions.question: What is the subject's religious orientation? One of: religious-practicing, spiritual-non-practicing, secular, agnostic, atheist.
questions.notes: From religious events, location patterns, content.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary language? Provide the ISO 639-1 code (e.g., en, es, bn, zh).
questions.notes: Direct from message content.

questions.question: What is the subject's marital / partnership status? One of: single, dating, engaged, married, separated, divorced, widowed.
questions.notes: From contacts, calendar, anchor relationships.

questions.question: Does the subject have children? Y or N.
questions.notes: From calendar (school events), contacts, messages.

questions.question: Has the subject moved residence within the last 24 months? Y or N.
questions.notes: From address-change patterns, life-phase language.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary phone OS? One of: iOS, Android.
questions.notes: Often direct from device-name events.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary computer OS? One of: macOS, Windows, Linux, ChromeOS.
questions.notes: From browser strings, sync events, search platform.

questions.question: What is the subject's OPSEC sophistication? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From VPN usage signals, search behaviour, account hygiene.

questions.question: Which cloud-sync provider does the subject use most? One of: Apple-iCloud, Google, Microsoft, Dropbox, mixed, none.
questions.notes: From sync events, account references.

questions.question: What is the subject's notification-fatigue level? One of: low (reads most), moderate, high (mostly unread).
questions.notes: From response latency, read/unread patterns.

questions.question: What is the subject's Openness (Big-Five)? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From content variety, novelty-seeking signals.

questions.question: What is the subject's Conscientiousness? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From schedule regularity, task completion.

questions.question: What is the subject's Extraversion? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From social-event density, message initiation.

questions.question: What is the subject's Agreeableness? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From conflict patterns, message tone.

questions.question: What is the subject's Neuroticism? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From anxiety signals, late-night activity, health searches.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary MICE+V exploitation lever? One of: M (Money), I (Ideology), C (Coercion/Compromise), E (Ego), V (Vice).
questions.notes: Most important for the threat model.

questions.question: What is the subject's secondary MICE+V lever? One of: M, I, C, E, V.
questions.notes: Should differ from primary.

questions.question: List up to 3 specific phobias or paranoias of the subject, as short phrases.
questions.notes: Word-level Jaccard over significant tokens (tolerates phrasing differences).

questions.question: List up to 3 specific guilt or shame triggers of the subject, as short phrases.
questions.notes: Word-level Jaccard over significant tokens.

questions.question: What is the subject's chronotype? One of: lark (early), neutral, owl (late).
questions.notes: Direct from activity timestamps.

questions.question: What is the subject's work-schedule pattern? One of: 9-5, hybrid, shifts, erratic, self-employed.
questions.notes: From calendar regularity, location patterns.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary commute mode? One of: car, public-transit, walk-bike, work-from-home, mixed.
questions.notes: From location-event durations, transit-related events.

questions.question: How much does the subject's weekend differ from weekday? One of: low, moderate, high.
questions.notes: From day-of-week activity patterns.

questions.question: During which time window does the subject most often doomscroll / engage in idle phone use? One of: morning, midday, afternoon, evening, late-night.
questions.notes: From low-intent activity timestamps.

questions.question: Does the subject have recurring focus blocks (windows with no digital activity)? Y or N.
questions.notes: From sustained activity gaps in calendar.

questions.question: What is the subject's household structure? One of: lives-alone, partner-only, nuclear-with-kids, single-parent, extended-family, blended, roommates.
questions.notes: From contact patterns, anchor relationships, calendar events.

questions.question: Name up to 3 of the subject's top anchor contacts (closest relationships, by first name or label).
questions.notes: Word-level Jaccard tolerates 'Sarah' vs 'sarah (fiancée)'.

questions.question: What is the relationship type of the subject's closest anchor contact? One of: spouse-partner, parent, sibling, child, friend, colleague, therapist, mentor.
questions.notes: From conversation content, frequency, contexts.

questions.question: Does the subject have a hidden contact, and if so what category? One of: affair, dealer, whistleblower, pseudonym, illicit-business, abuse-confidant, none.
questions.notes: Recovering this from the bundle alone is hard; a high score here indicates the bundle is unusually revealing.

questions.question: Is the subject in a consistent home time-zone, or do they travel / live across zones? One of: consistent, occasional-travel, frequent-travel, nomadic.
questions.notes: From location time-zones over the period.

questions.question: What is the subject's dominant communication tone? One of: formal, casual, sarcastic, cynical, warm, mixed.
questions.notes: From message content; subjective.

questions.question: What is the subject's specific job title? Short phrase (e.g., 'DevOps engineer', 'ICU nurse', 'middle-school teacher').
questions.notes: Semantic match: same field + same seniority = 1.0; same field different seniority = 0.5; different field = 0.0.

questions.question: Does the subject regularly produce visible typos or non-standard grammar in messages? Y or N.
questions.notes: From message corpus.

questions.question: At approximately what hour (24h) does the subject typically wake on weekdays? Provide an integer 0-23.
questions.notes: From first-event-of-day timestamps.

questions.question: What is the subject's highest completed education level? One of: no-degree, high-school, associate, bachelor, master, doctorate.
questions.notes: From explicit mentions of alma mater, degrees, professional credentials, or implied by job title.

questions.question: List the subject's top 2-3 hobbies or recurring leisure interests (e.g. running, guitar, gaming, gardening). Free-form short phrases.
questions.notes: From weekend activity patterns, recurring calendar events, subscriptions, search history. Distinct from Q25 phobias (fears) and Q26 guilt triggers (shame).

questions.question: Does the subject have a disclosed or strongly-implied health condition (physical or mental)? If yes, give a short label (e.g. 'anxiety', 'diabetes', 'chronic pain'); if not, answer 'none-disclosed'.
questions.notes: From medication purchases, doctor visits, therapy mentions, explicit anxiety/depression references. Distinct from Q25 (cognitive phobias) and Q26 (moral guilt) — Q44 is about clinical/physical states.

questions.question: What is the subject's spending tier for discretionary purchases? One of: budget, mid-range, premium, luxury, mixed, or unknown-cannot-determine if the activity does not show enough signal.
questions.notes: Infer from search queries (brand names, price comparison), media (retailer apps), location visits (mall tier), messages mentioning purchases. Distinct from Q05 SES tier which is broader.

questions.question: What is the subject's primary purchase channel? One of: online-mostly, brick-and-mortar-mostly, mixed, minimal-shopper, or unknown-cannot-determine.
questions.notes: Online: Amazon/eBay app use, package-pickup calendar events. Brick-and-mortar: store location visits, retail-zone clustering.

questions.question: Where does the subject spend most discretionary money? One of: fashion, tech, wellness-fitness, home-decor, experiences-travel, food-dining, gaming-entertainment, minimal, mixed, or unknown-cannot-determine.
questions.notes: Strongest signal in searches + videos (unboxings, reviews) + media (apps used) + locations (gyms, restaurants, electronics stores).

questions.question: Briefly describe the subject's general physical appearance — a short phrase like 'tall slim glasses', 'average build short hair', or 'unknown' if insufficient signal in the activity. The activity is TEXT-ONLY (no images), so abstaining is encouraged when signal is weak.
questions.notes: Weak signal expected. Possible cues: gym visits (build), hair-product searches, eyewear retailer visits (glasses), clothing brand mentions (size hints). Designed to test text-only inference floor.
